title: gh-144763: Don't detach the GIL in tracemalloc
author: vstinner
body:
tracemalloc no longer detachs the GIL to acquire its internal lock.

<!--

Please read this comment in its entirety. It's quite important.

Most PRs will require an issue number. Trivial changes, like fixing a typo, do not need an issue.

* Issue: gh-144763
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
This change basically reverts commit 01157e0cdff1321a065b0a00c5a97460f52af07e (PR gh-139449) which fixed another deadlock on `tracemalloc.stop()` :-) See issue gh-139116.

cc @ZeroIntensity @encukou

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
> This change basically reverts commit https://github.com/python/cpython/commit/01157e0cdff1321a065b0a00c5a97460f52af07e (PR https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/139449) which fixed another deadlock on tracemalloc.stop() :-) See issue https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/139116.

I pushed a change to avoid the deadlock on `tracemalloc.stop()`: don't call `PyRefTracer_SetTracer()` while holding `TABLES_LOCK()`.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
> Is it necessary to keep the attached thread state in TABLES_LOCK? It seems the bulk of the fix is just calling PyRefTracer_SetTracer without holding the lock.

There are two problems:

* (A) detaching the thread state in TABLES_LOCK() breaks a lot of assumptions in the `_io` module about critical sections, the GC and memory allocators leading to race conditions. See https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144763#issuecomment-3896179544 for the problem on the `_io.TextIOWrapper.write()` method.
* (B) Deadlock involving `PyRefTracer_SetTracer()` in `tracemalloc.stop()`: see issue gh-139116. My fix is to call `PyRefTracer_SetTracer()` without holding `TABLES_LOCK()` (which is safe at this point in `_PyTraceMalloc_Stop()`).

Yes, I think that it's necessary to keep the attached thread state in TABLES_LOCK (not detach the thread state).

> Alternatively, we could just use Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION_MUTEX instead of PyMutex_Lock.

What would be the benefits?

comments.author: colesbury
comments.body:
Using `_Py_LOCK_DONT_DETACH` in `TABLES_LOCK()` definitely seems like the right approach for the allocator calls (i.e., `tracemalloc_alloc`/`tracemalloc_free`).  We don't want to detach the thread state there.

I'm not really sure about `_PyTraceMalloc_Stop()` and `_PyTraceMalloc_Start()`. Should these be safe to call concurrently? The implementation isn't thread-safe for concurrent calls to `tracemalloc.start()` or `tracemalloc.stop()`. Maybe that's fine?

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
> I'm not really sure about _PyTraceMalloc_Stop() and _PyTraceMalloc_Start(). Should these be safe to call concurrently? The implementation isn't thread-safe for concurrent calls to tracemalloc.start() or tracemalloc.stop(). Maybe that's fine?

I'm only aware of https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/128679 : concurrent calls to `PyTraceMalloc_Track()` and `tracemalloc.stop()` (case tested by `test_tracemalloc.test_tracemalloc_track_race()`).

I don't think that two threads should be able to call `tracemalloc.stop()` and `tracemalloc.start()` in parallel. I expect that only a single thread (the main thread?) would call these two methods.

comments.author: ZeroIntensity
comments.body: Could we use `_Py_LOCK_DONT_DETACH` in `TABLES_LOCK`, but use a normal (detaching) call in `start`/`stop`? That should avoid the reftracer deadlock without needing to move the call to `PyRefTracer_SetTracer`.

comments.author: ZeroIntensity
comments.body:
Nevermind, that would lead to a lock-ordering deadlock:

1. Thread A releases the GIL and acquires `TABLES_LOCK`.
2. Thread B acquires the GIL and begins waiting on `TABLES_LOCK`.
3. Thread A is waiting on the GIL, which thread B holds.
4. Deadlock!

If I remember correctly, `tracemalloc` still needs more work to avoid crashes when running concurrently (particularly in subinterpreters), so I think it would be fine to leave `start`/`stop` as is in this PR and address it later.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: @kumaraditya303 @colesbury @ZeroIntensity: So what do you think of this change? Does it look good to you?

comments.author: bedevere-app[bot]
comments.body: [GH-144965](https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/144965) is a backport of this pull request to the [3.14 branch](https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/3.14).

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: Backport of the fix to 3.14: https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/144965. The backport is different since the 3.14 code already avoided to detach the thread safe.
